questions.question: What is the primary goal of Test-Driven Development (TDD)?
options: B) To write more efficient code
C) To write testable code first
questions.explanation: TDD is a software development process that relies on the repetitive cycle of writing automated tests before writing the actual code.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a good practice in software engineering?
options: A) Using complex algorithms to solve simple problems
B) Writing comments to explain complex code
C) Using version control to track changes
questions.explanation: Writing comments to explain complex code helps other developers understand the codebase and maintain it over time.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP) in software engineering?
options: C) To ensure a class has only one reason to change
questions.explanation: SRP states that a class should have only one reason to change, which helps in maintaining and evolving the codebase.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key benefit of using Design Patterns?
questions.explanation: Design Patterns provide a proven solution to common problems, making the codebase more maintainable and efficient.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using Continuous Integration (CI) in software development?
questions.explanation: CI involves automating the build, test, and deployment process, which helps in catching errors early, improving code quality, and reducing deployment time.
